findings.title: Delegated-review docs gate can false-pass when runtime backend doc is absent
findings.rationale: `runtime_backend_requirement` defaults to true when `docs/runtime-backend.md` is missing (`map_or(true, ...)`), so `runtime_backend_forbids_parent_only_gate_result` can pass without any runtime-backend wording being present. That creates a release-gate false positive for the delegated-review authority contract.
findings.suggested_next_action: Require the runtime-backend check to fail or skip (not pass) when the document is missing, and only pass when normalized content proves the required parent-vs-reviewer authority text.

findings.title: Delegated-review docs phrase check is newline-fragile and can false-fail
findings.rationale: `internal_orchestration_delegates_judgment` matches the literal substring `not review\njudgment authority`. A markdown reflow to single-line text or different wrapping would fail this check even if semantics are unchanged, creating false negatives in qualification. The same function already normalizes whitespace for runtime-backend wording, so this is inconsistent and brittle.
findings.suggested_next_action: Normalize whitespace for internal-orchestration and review-loop wording checks before matching semantic phrases, and avoid literal newline-dependent substrings in required-doc contract gates.

findings.title: Control-loop boundary gate depends on message substrings instead of stable semantic fields
findings.rationale: The new `control_loop_boundary` pass criteria rely on human-readable `systemMessage`/`reason` fragments (for example `Ralph loop is not active` and `review gate`). Behavioral-preserving wording changes can therefore fail the gate, which raises false-negative risk for release qualification.
findings.suggested_next_action: Prefer machine-stable decision markers in stop-hook output (for example explicit reason codes/flags) and make gate checks key off those structured fields rather than free-text phrases.
